target.name: Netrasemi
target.founder: Jyothis Indirabhai, Deepa Geetha, Sreejith Varma

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Netrasemi is a Series A chip design company founded by Jyothis Indirabhai, Deepa Geetha, and Sreejith Varma, having raised approximately $13M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 4 private peers in the Chips & Semiconductors sector at the same or adjacent stage, including Substrate ($1.0B, Series A), Rain AI ($500M, Series A), Positron AI ($1.0B, Series B), and Etched ($5.0B, Series B). A public comp set of 3 semiconductor tickers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) with a median P/S of 20.3x exists but cannot be applied without disclosed revenue.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach. The median valuation across the 4 private comparables is $1.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. This method anchors the core valuation signal given Netrasemi's direct stage and sector alignment with Substrate and Rain AI.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $850M to $1.1B, centered on $1.0B. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data (limiting public-comp validation), product differentiation relative to the peer set, and the wide dispersion in the private comp range ($500M to $5.0B), which reflects stage variance and heterogeneous investor conviction within the frontier chip sector. Validation would benefit from revenue multiples or customer traction metrics once disclosed.
